name: Maria Polletta
story_relevance_note: AZCIR is the AZ-based nonprofit investigative outlet most likely to dig into the State Board of Education's educator discipline pipeline. As managing editor, Polletta sets the assignment list and would be the primary contact for offering the data set behind the 325-discipline-actions investigation, including the 36% sexual misconduct figure tied to Maricopa County survivors.

name: Adriana Navarro
role: Investigative Reporter / Roy W. Howard Fellow
story_relevance_note: AZCIR's Howard Fellow Navarro covers AZ accountability stories. The educator discipline data set, with named survivors documented through ARS 12-514's 12-year SOL window, fits her investigative remit on institutional failures protecting Arizona children.
personalization_hook: Saw your analysis of Republican lawmakers' bills and court actions targeting Proposition 139 in AZCIR.org.

name: Jasmine Demers
story_relevance_note: Demers covers health and child welfare overlap. Educator sexual misconduct against AZ K-12 students is a child trauma + public health story, including survivor mental health outcomes and barriers to disclosure within Arizona's 180-day Notice of Claim deadline under ARS 12-821.01.
personalization_hook: Saw your piece on Arizona's $2.5 billion Medicaid fraud scandal targeting Indigenous communities.

name: Molly Bohannon
story_relevance_note: AZCIR's education beat reporter. The State Board of Education revoked or denied 325 educator credentials in 2024, with Maricopa County districts driving the volume. Bohannon's higher-ed credential beat extends naturally to K-12 educator licensing failures.
personalization_hook: Saw your article on undocumented students facing in-state tuition challenges under Proposition 308 in the Arizona Daily Star.

name: Ally Markovich
story_relevance_note: Markovich covers AZ criminal justice. Educator sexual misconduct cases against students often run parallel criminal prosecutions plus civil actions against districts. The 36% sexual misconduct share of decade discipline cases is a criminal-justice + child-safety crossover story.

name: AZCIR Newsroom
story_relevance_note: AZCIR's public newsroom contact for tips. The AZ State Board of Education's 325 disciplinary actions in a single calendar year, plus the multi-year Maricopa County concentration, is a tippable accountability data set.

name: Sarah Lapidus
story_relevance_note: Lapidus is the Republic's K-12 education reporter and the most natural byline for an AZ State Board of Education discipline-pipeline story. Maricopa County district concentration in the 325-actions data lands squarely in her coverage area.

name: Helen Rummel
story_relevance_note: Rummel covers AZ education and breaking news for the Republic. The 36% sexual misconduct share of decade educator discipline cases, with named Maricopa County districts in the data, is a publishable accountability piece for her beat.
personalization_hook: Saw your reporting with Taylor Seely on Arizona's higher education leadership principles statement in November 2025.

name: Sarah Henry
story_relevance_note: Henry is on the Republic's AZ news staff masthead. Survivor-first framing of educator misconduct cases tied to the AZ State Board's 2024 discipline numbers fits her general AZ reporting posture.

name: Logan Stanley
story_relevance_note: Stanley is on the Republic's AZ newsroom staff page. Investigation includes a public records dossier on AZ State Board enforcement leads David Spelich, Garnett Burns, and Bruce Duplanty that's tippable for any AZ news byline.

name: Taylor Seely
story_relevance_note: Seely covers Phoenix-area accountability beats for the Republic. Maricopa County's outsize share of the 325-action educator discipline data is a Phoenix-metro accountability story.

name: Katherine Fritcke
story_relevance_note: Listed on the Republic's staff masthead. Survivor-first reporting on educator credential revocations, plus the 12-year ARS 12-514 SOL window for child sexual abuse claims, is publishable AZ accountability journalism.

name: AZ State Board of Education Investigative Unit
story_relevance_note: This is the official AZ State Board of Education email channel for educator discipline investigations, the unit that drove the 325 disciplinary actions in 2024. Direct contact for response on enforcement staff pipeline (Spelich, Burns, Stolarow, Ross, Duplanty) and on the 36% sexual misconduct share of decade cases.
personalization_hook: Saw your "Enforcement Action Report 2023" detailing the Investigative Unit's process for complex complaint cases and PPAC recommendations.

name: AZ Department of Education School Safety + Social Wellness
story_relevance_note: ADE's official School Safety inbox. The educator discipline pipeline is the upstream signal of where school safety failed Arizona K-12 students. Survivor-first inquiry about systemic prevention measures across Maricopa County districts.
personalization_hook: Saw your School Safety Program's grant funding for 2023/2024 through 2025/2026 to place school re.

name: AZ Department of Education Constituent Services
outlet: Arizona Department of Education / Tom Horne press office
story_relevance_note: Public ADE inquiry channel under Superintendent Tom Horne's office. Press response on the 325-action 2024 dataset, the 36% sexual misconduct figure, and ADE's role in Maricopa County district accountability.

name: AZ Department of Education Communications Office
story_relevance_note: ADE's official press office. Direct route for Tom Horne response on the AZ State Board's 2024 discipline volume and the multi-year Maricopa County concentration of educator misconduct cases.

name: J.J. Rico
story_relevance_note: Disability Rights AZ is the federally-designated AZ Protection and Advocacy organization for K-12 students with disabilities, who face elevated risk from educator misconduct. Rico leads the org and would have institutional perspective on the survivor population overrepresented in the 325-action discipline pipeline.
personalization_hook: Saw your comments condemning the White House's "Ending Crime and Disorder on America’s Streets" executive order on mental health and homelessness.

name: Natalie Luna Rose
story_relevance_note: Senior Disability Rights AZ staff with public-facing intake role. AZ K-12 students with disabilities are a survivor population disproportionately affected by educator misconduct documented in the State Board's discipline data.
personalization_hook: Saw your contact role for reasonable accommodations on DRAZ's Compliance, Oversight, Monitoring, and Investigations Team (COMIT) job posting.

name: ACESDV
outlet: Arizona Coalition to End Sexual and Domestic Violence
story_relevance_note: ACESDV is the AZ statewide coalition serving sexual violence survivors. The 36% sexual misconduct share of decade educator discipline cases tracks survivor populations they serve, including K-12 student survivors navigating Arizona's 12-year ARS 12-514 SOL and 180-day ARS 12-821.01 Notice of Claim windows.
